behaviors.description: Error message names the missing required key by its exact config path when absent from both file and environment
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is in the error-message formatting logic: does the validator correctly identify and name the missing key path (e.g. `database.host`)? This logic operates on the already-merged config dict and env dict — no cross-module wiring or I/O is needed to reproduce it. Calling the validation function with a crafted dict missing a nested key is sufficient to catch the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the config validator directly with a parsed config dict that has `database.host` absent and an env dict with no matching `TOOL_*` override. Assert the raised exception message contains the exact string `database.host`.

behaviors.description: Loader resolves config file path relative to XDG_CONFIG_HOME when that variable is set
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the path-computation logic: does the resolver read `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and build the path from it instead of hardcoding `~/.config`? This is a deterministic function of an env var and a constant suffix — no real filesystem access is required to verify the computed path. Setting `os.environ['XDG_CONFIG_HOME']` in-process and calling the path-resolver function is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Patch `os.environ` so `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set to a known string (e.g. `/tmp/xdg`). Call the config-path resolver function. Assert the returned path starts with `/tmp/xdg` and ends with `tool/config.yaml`, and does not contain `.config`.

behaviors.description: Environment variable with TOOL_ prefix overrides the corresponding key from the config file
behaviors.justification: The failure is entirely within the merge/override function: given a base config dict and an env dict, does the env value win? This is pure algorithmic logic with no I/O or cross-module interaction. A single call to the merge function with crafted inputs is enough to expose the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the config-merge function with a base config dict containing `{"database": {"host": "file-host"}}` and an env dict `{"TOOL_DATABASE_HOST": "env-host"}`. Assert the returned merged config has `database.host == "env-host"`.

behaviors.description: Error message includes the line number when the config file contains a YAML syntax error
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the exception-handling wrapper around `yaml.safe_load`: does it extract and surface the line number from the parser's exception? This requires actually parsing malformed YAML (so the real PyYAML exception with line info is raised), but a YAML string can be passed directly to the loader function — no filesystem or subprocess needed. Mocking the YAML parser would hide whether line-number extraction is wired correctly, but the real parser can be invoked in-process on a string.
behaviors.test_description: Craft a YAML string with a deliberate syntax error on line 3 (e.g. two valid lines then a bad indent/tab mix). Pass it as a string (or `io.StringIO`) to the config-file parser function. Assert the raised error message contains `"3"` (the line number reported by PyYAML).

behaviors.description: Loader creates the config directory and writes a defaults file when the directory does not exist at startup
behaviors.justification: The failure mode spans at least two operations that must succeed in order: (1) detect the directory is absent, (2) create it with `os.makedirs`, (3) write the defaults file. Mocking any of these would prevent detecting bugs in their interaction (e.g. file written to wrong path, created with wrong permissions, or defaults content is wrong). A real temporary directory gives the filesystem feedback needed. This is Integration (in-process real I/O via a temp dir), not System, because no subprocess or external network call is required.
behaviors.test_description: Using `tmp_path` (pytest's temp-directory fixture), compute a nonexistent subdirectory path. Call the startup initializer with that path as the config home. After it returns, assert the directory exists on the real filesystem and that a defaults file (e.g. `config.yaml`) is present inside it with the expected default keys.
